i_L(t) \approx
\begin{cases}
0.8 t - 2 \times 10^{-6}\ \text{A}, & t \le 1 \times 10^{-6}\ \text{sec} \\[4pt]
-0.2 t - 1 \times 10^{-4}\ \text{A}, & 1 \times 10^{-6} \le t \le 3 \times 10^{-6}\ \text{sec} \\[4pt]
1.6 \times 10^{-4}\ \text{A}, & t \ge 3 \times 10^{-6}\ \text{sec}
\end{cases}
$$
Rectified Version:
$$
i_L(t) =
\begin{cases}
(0.8t - 2 \times 10^{-6}) \, \text{A}, & t < 1 \times 10^{-6} \, \text{sec} \\
(-0.2t - 1 \times 10^{-6}) \, \text{A}, & 1 \times 10^{-6} \le t < 3 \times 10^{-6} \, \text{sec} \\
1.6 \times 10^{-6} \, \text{A}, & t \ge 3 \times 10^{-6} \, \text{sec}
\end{cases}
$$
Reason: There are multiple errors in the piecewise function:
- The constant term in the second case is `-1 \times 10^{-4}` instead of `-1 \times 10^{-6}`.
- The value in the third case is `1.6 \times 10^{-4}` instead of `1.6 \times 10^{-6}`.
- The boundary conditions for the first and second cases use `≤` where the ground truth uses `<`.
- The relation is `≈` instead of `=`.
